verification_gate: A selected database version deterministically reconstructs paths, bytes, metadata, ordering, permissions, and module relationships; repeated projections are byte-identical; projection output is disposable and traceable to database identities.

selected_database_version.determinism_rule: selection is by explicit pinned identity, never mutable 'latest'; a selector resolves to a fixed immutable row set
selected_database_version.source: Architecture Blueprint materialization step 1 (final production branch); Context Matrix row 25 (module_path metadata, production branch ID)

deterministically_reconstructs.paths: module_path metadata -> directory hierarchy; verified by sorted path-set hash equality
deterministically_reconstructs.bytes: ordered raw_code blocks -> exact-byte concatenation; verified by composed BlobStore SHA-256 equality (Exact-byte materialization, ADOPT-001 PR#16)
deterministically_reconstructs.metadata: module_path/language/status columns; verified by captured_metadata_json_projection_is_stable (SHA-256 identity, ADOPT-001 PR#14)
deterministically_reconstructs.ordering: stored block ordinal + canonical file/dir enumeration; verified by whole-tree byte-stream equality
deterministically_reconstructs.permissions: stored file-mode + symlink-limit metadata; verified by materialization_paths.rs fail-closed mode/symlink checks
deterministically_reconstructs.module_relationships: module_path hierarchy + causal dependency edges -> compilable module graph; verified by relationship-graph hash equality and downstream musl compile

repeated_projections_byte_identical.rule: two projections of the same pinned version, on any conforming host, produce a byte-identical output tree
repeated_projections_byte_identical.verification_method: project pinned version twice into disposable targets; assert whole-tree SHA-256 workspace identity equality
repeated_projections_byte_identical.evidence_primitive: SHA-256 identity / workspace_identity parity (ADOPT-001 PR#14, CDB106 green)

projection_output_disposable.rule: output holds no authority; deletable with zero loss; regenerated wholesale from the pinned database version
projection_output_disposable.manual_edit_policy: manual edits to generated output are not merged and do not survive re-projection (goal: 'no manual edits to generated output')

traceable_to_database_identities.verification_method: recompute composed SHA-256 per file from ordered block hashes; assert equal to on-disk hash and that every hash resolves to a real PostgreSQL/BlobStore row
traceable_to_database_identities.evidence_primitive: envctl export contract (ADOPT-001 PR#19, CDB035) + Cryptographic Witness Chain lineage (Architecture Blueprint section 6)

engine_never: writes durable state back to PostgreSQL from output
treats output files as source of truth
no_reverse_ownership_ambiguity.store_001_invariant: single durable writer preserved; projection is a read path, not a durable owner

store_001_alignment.governing_state_class: derived projections (materialized source directories for musl compilation, exported release trees)
store_001_alignment.canonical_owner: PostgreSQL (source of truth) projected by envctl / CodeDB materialization

proof_summary: POSTGRES-008 defines envctl as the deterministic, read-only, forward-only projection engine that reconstructs a selected PostgreSQL branch and pinned version into a clean, disposable directory. generated/envctl_materialization_plan.json binds a pinned selection contract (production_branch_id + version_snapshot_id + status='production') to the blueprint's four-stage pipeline (Query -> Map -> Concatenate -> Dump) and assigns every facet the gate enumerates exactly once with a database source, a pure reconstruction rule, and a verification check: paths from module_path metadata, bytes from ordered raw_code blocks via exact-byte concatenation, metadata from module_path/language/status columns, ordering from stored block ordinals plus canonical enumeration, permissions from stored file-mode and symlink-limit metadata, and module relationships from the module_path hierarchy plus causal dependency edges. Byte-identical repeat projection is established by a whole-tree SHA-256 workspace-identity comparison; disposability by delete-and-re-project with unchanged canonical rows and a manual-edit-discard policy; traceability by a projection manifest mapping every emitted file to its branch/version/module_path and ordered content-addressed block hashes. Authority is one-directional: PostgreSQL is canonical, the projected directory is derived, so there is no reverse ownership edge and generated output is never source, aligning with STORE-001's derived-projections class (write_authority = envctl projection read-only from PostgreSQL; recovery_source = re-projection) and its single-durable-writer invariant. Every assignment is grounded in the Architecture Blueprint materialization steps, the Context Matrix rows for materialization and ingestion, and the adopted proven capabilities Exact-byte materialization (PR#16), SHA-256 identity (PR#14), envctl export contract (PR#19), and BlobStore (PR#13/#14). The live re-projection parity run against a provisioned PostgreSQL and cross-OS permission parity are recorded as open dependencies. No product execution, no live materialization, and no database write were performed.
